item.title: Tool server connections default to admin-only since OpenClaw v0.8.7 — blueprints with tools are invisible to users
item.intent: Every blueprint that exposes tools must generate explicit access grants so tools are visible to users. The v0.8.7 default (no grants = admin-only) silently breaks all existing blueprints on upgrade.
item.description: OpenClaw v0.8.7 changed the default access model for tool server and terminal connections: when no explicit access grants are set, connections are now private (admin-only). Previously they were visible to all users. ClawHQ's config generator (src/design/configure/generate.ts) does not set explicit access grants for blueprint tools, meaning any agent running on v0.8.7+ will have tools invisible to non-admin users. Fix: the config generator must emit explicit access grants for all tools a blueprint defines. This should also be added as a doctor check — detect v0.8.7+ and verify tool access grants are present.
acceptanceCriteria: Config generator emits explicit access grants for all tools defined in a blueprint
An agent forged from any blueprint on OpenClaw v0.8.7+ has tools visible to the configured user
clawhq doctor detects missing tool access grants and reports a warning with fix suggestion
Existing config generation tests pass with no regression
